questions.text: Have safety procedures been evaluated to ensure they are compliant with OH&S regulations?
Does site manager review lagging OH&S KPIs (e.g. number of incidents, frequency, severity) at least monthly?
Are the root causes of incidents categorized and then tracked?
Are there repercussions/consequences for teammates that do not follow the Safety Rules and Procedures?
Does site manager follow and review "Leading indicators" in their safety result tracking?
When creating the annual (or more) safety implementation plan, do you consider the KPI result/progress in previous year?
Do you motivate teammates to find and implement new best practices relevant to safety in other BS sites?
Does the site management team benchmark Safety and DP trends in logistics industry / other external companies and consider incorporation into site targets/activities?

Have you monitored the H&S and DP regulations* which may be applicable for your site and checked that these regulations have been taken place in your sites? (e.g. trainings, mobile equipment, chemicals, flammable materials)
Are safety initiatives being implemented to improve safety? Is there any document to show that?
Do you know how important teammates' involvement in safety is?　Besides, do you explain the importance to teammates and involve them in safety activities?
Is there a H&S meeting with representation from teammates, leadership and site H&S (if available)? Does leadership give directions to solve issues?
Can you show examples of implemented safety measures based on teammate feedback?
Have you gathered best practices on safety activity from GBS/SBU, and implement them in the site?
Does your site manager consider safety a priority and address safety at the morning assembly, roll-call, etc.?
What does site leadership (e.g. site manager and supervisor) do when a serious incident  (lost-time-injury, fire accident, etc.) occurs?
Does Site leaders actively provide feedback to teammates and promote safety whilst on tours?
What is your responsibility for safety?
Do you recognize good safety behaviors of teammates and give them rewards?
Does management have a mid-term plan including Safety and DP items and their necessary resources to improve safety on site?
Is there an external/cross site safety meeting? If so, does safety manager share H&S issues or best practices?
Do you feel comfortable stopping work if you feel there is a risk of injury?
What are H&S risks relevant to your work? What makes you recognize them?
Are you aware of your personal impact on safety? How do you contribute to improve safety?
Do teammates know how to report incidents?
What are the most important safety rules in your site/job and why?
Do you understand the greatest potential risks in your work, and how they are controlled?
How do you encourage communication between sites to share best-practices and lessons-learned?
What would be considered a priority: complete the operation plan or follow safety procedures?
What actions do you take during your operations to prevent reoccurrence of an incident which occurred in your site?
Are the site/job safety rules being followed?
Have teammates proposed improvements of H&S risks and risk mitigation measures at the creations or revisions of work standards, training materials, etc?
What response would you expect from your colleague when you act unsafe?
What requirement, based on safety regulations do you explain to teammate?
What incidents and the countermeasures at your site did you receive from managements recently?
Are you regularly trained about your site/job safety rules?
Have you taken training on residual risks in your work? How are you trained?
Are there opportunities to share H&S risks and countermeasures of other sites at the safety meeting in your site?
Is the legally required H&S training given to all teammates?
Are incident investigations carried out by qualified people?
Are safety rule's effectiveness being evaluated by the H&S (if available), teammates and their site leaders?
Do work/job specific trainings highlight safety risks and processes associated with that work/job?
Is risk assessment taken when planning new projects/work sessions?
Do teammates communicate injuries/near-misses/defects to managers, and does management follow up them until close?
Are H&S/DP rules communicated to teammates when updated/changed/newly created.
Do you know what safety initiatives are on going on site and how they are performing?
When a safety issue would occur, who would you report to?
What is a content of system and a scope for "management of change"?
Do you communicate with OH&S expert about upcoming changes on site when there are any concerns on Safety?
What was the feedback from management on your hazard report?
Do you receive feedback from third party on effectiveness of safety management system to imorove it?
Does management have defined roles and responsibilities for safety activities?
Is there collaboration between Logistics safety dept. and site management to address recurring incidents?
Do safety training sessions for newcomers reference relevant frequent incidents and severe risks?
Are safety processes updated and improved frequently using findings from the review?
Have you involved TMs in periodical review on safety action plans?
Do teammates comply with safety rules and improve risks actively being conscious of safety for not only their selves, but also teammates. ?
Do you engage with *external parties to obtain and exchange　knowledge on safety?
Have teammates been trained on the safety mission statement and high risk activity?
Are you aware of the regulations concerning safety training?
Have role specific trainings been identified and are given to the necessary people?
Are the needs and the content of trainings reviewed and improved after an accident occurs?
Are trainings reviewed and adjusted when safety rules and controls that involve that specific subject matter change?
When new hazards are identified, are training material revised and communicated to teammates?
Are trainings updated with industry best practices?
Is the effectiveness of a training reviewed based on pre and post training assessments? Is the training improved based on the assessment result?
Are the roles & responsibilities of emergency organization defined in the emergency response policy/procedure that complies with legal & local regulations?
Do emergency drills periodically take place?
Are the emergency procedures reviewed and updated based on the results of drills or actual emergencies?
Do emergency response plans which have clear rules and guidelines address each potential scenarios?
Have the drills been conducted by every shifts and cover each identified potential scenarios?
Are best practices in other sites incorporated into the procedure for emergency?
When implementation of regular OH&S communication and OH&S training, does the site manager use the language where the teammates are familiar?
Are the causes of accidents/incidents communicated to teammates?
Are site safety rules communicated to workers in a clear and structured manner?
Does management inform workers of newly found hazards and risks in a timely manner?
Do teammates communicate hazards and risks to their supervisors as soon as they identify them?
Do you communicate improvements with your TMs to evaluate the effectiveness against unsafe conditions or near misses that TMs have reported?
Do you conduct the LOTO training to the teammates who perform servicing or maintenance tasks, and distribute the Lock-out & Tag-out devices?
Is the LOTO standard reviewed after incidents, audits or opportunity areas have been identified?
Is the LOTO system being used and applied by teammates performing maintenance tasks?
Is priodical monitoring on LOTO activity performed to verify that LOTO are properly implemented when required?
Are ECPs (Energy Control Procedures) prepared for all applicable machinery? Do they include all applicable energy sources (electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, etc.)?
Have you gathered best practices on LOTO from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Do tyou know the safety rules that are required to be followed? 　(8 common safety rules, mobile equipment rules, crane & hoist rules and tire inflation rules applying to his/her job position)
Is the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents, audits or opportunity areas have been identified?
Are the applicable BS common safety rules followed by the teammates?
Have you ever engaged to manage and evaluate the risks involved in their work?
Are safety rules developed/adjusted after these evaluations take place?
Is feedback from teammates considered to improve safety rules?
Have you gathered best practices on safety rules(e.g. work standards) from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Have you communicated Work at height requirements to teammates performing Work at height?
What are the countermeasures against a incident involving work at height which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
Are Work at height controls being used and applied by teammates performing tasks that require Work at height?
Are Work at height controls reviewed and improved after new risks are identified?
Have you gathered best practices on safety rules(e.g. work standards) from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Have you communicated requirements on mobile equipment to the drivers?
What are the countermeasures against a incident involving mobile equipment which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
Do all drivers follow the rules and procedures for mobile equipment?
Are Mobile Equipment controls reviewed and iproved after new risks are identified?
Is feedback from drivers considered when adapting the mobile equipment standard?
Have you gathered best practices on mobile equipment from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Has the need for 3S(Select & remove, Sort, Keep clean) in the work area been communicated?
Are 3S visual standards displayed in work area?
Have 3S leaders been assigned from shop floor workers and have 3S self check by 3S leaders been periodically performed?
Are all unnecessary objects being removed or planned to be removed from the work area ?
Are necessary equipment/tools being put in clearly marked and fixed positions?
Do teammates proceed with 3S activity for the improvement of 3S level?
Have you gathered best practices on 3S from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
How do you identify and ensure compliance to crane and hoist relevant safety legislation ?
Is there an established crane and hoist safety standard that complies with legal requirements?
Is there a list of licensed crane operators?
What are the countermeasures against a hoist & crane incident which occurred in your site in last 1 year?
Do crane operators follow the rules and standards concerning cranes and hoist operations?
Do crane operators provide feedback (safety perspective) to their peers inside and outside of their site?
Have you gathered best practices on　crane and hoist from other sites/external companies, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Have the the process and the importance of KY been communicated to the teammates?
Have a KY leader and KY representatives been appointed?
Does everyone on site participate in KYT?
Are the results from KY used to develop new safety rules or adjust old rules or improvement of safety measures for hazards((risk mitigation/administrative measures)?
Is KYT conducted periodically in accordance with SBU or/and site standard by everyone on site?
Is the KYT activity evaluated and opportunity for improvement discussed with the site manager annually?
Have you gathered best practices and/or risks found in other sites during KY, and considered to incorporate them into site procedure?
Has the tire inflation standard been communicated to the teammates?
Is there a clearly marked area specifically for tire inflation?
Is basic safety equipment available in the inflation area?
Do workers in the tire inflation area follow the safety rules concerning tire inflation?
Do the workers know how to effectively mitigate the risks in the tire inflation process?
Have you gathered best practices on tire inflation from other sites/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure?
Do you certify if contractor employees who perform unload/load the tires have required skills? (e.g. mobile equipment)
Are pre-work meetings conducted prior to the start of work, are risks identified and risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined.
Do all contractor employees receive contractor H&S orientation/training prior to the start of the work?
Does the material of contractor H&S orientation/training includes emergency procedures, introduction to the Safety Mission Statement and Safety fundamental Activities?
Does BS perform safety patrols of contractor's work area, and confirm that risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined at the pre-work meeting are conducted?

Have you gathered best practices on contractor management from other sites/external companies, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure? (E.g. Daily JSA, using the external consultant for selecting contractors, etc.)
Are large tire manual handling operations identified and justified? Is there a procedure for each of these operations?
Has the Large Tire Manual Handling Operation procedure been communicated to the relevant people?
Is the large tire manual handling operation procedure reviewed in response to incidents?
Is the procedure followed accordingly by teammates when manually handling large tires?
Is the procedure reviewed when new activities are introduced on the site or when teammates offer relevant feedback?
Are teammates re-trained in the large tire manual handling operations procedure when required (e.g. returning from absence, changes in procedure)
Are best industry practices and new advances in large tire manual handling equipment considered to modify or improve procedures on site?
Have the OH&S and Fire risks in the site evaluated?
Does the risk evaluation address the foreseeable hazards related to machine?
Do you have implementation plan for risk control measures against risk category III and IV?
Does the risk evaluation address the foreseeable hazards related to chemical and ergonomics?
Are all risks of category III and IV in the risk register covered by administrative or technical controls?
Have risk assessment periodically reviewed to verify that hazards are definitely identified and risks are properly evaluated?
Are best industry practices or new technology considered to mitigate the OH&S and fire risks on site?
To whom do you report when incidents are identified?
Are the incidents that occurred at other sites (BS group or external) communicated?
Are countermeasures determined when incidents* occurred?
Are investigation conducted when you receive SBU recurrence prevention request ?
Is root cause analysis implemented when incidents* occurred?
Have countermeasures been implemented for incidents*?
Do you have implementation plan to take countermeasures for identified risks after investigation based on recurrence prevention request?
Are risk control measurements being executed to similar tasks, machines or processes following a reported incident*?
Have countermeasures been implemented based on implementation plan for recurrence prevention?
Are industry best practices being considered when determining the countermeasures against incidents*?
